label: Audit Checkpoint Anchor
info:
Polls the DCS audit-trail checkpoint head (ADR-16) and drops it on a debug node.

TODO: PIN THIS PROPERLY. A debug node anchors nothing, and evidence that never leaves the operator's reach proves nothing against the operator. Route the head to storage we do NOT control: a notary, a public chain, a counterparty - anything third-party and append-only.

Auth: ORCE is an SRS System User (SRS 2.4, Table 5). It authenticates against this deployment's Hydra with its own client_id/client_secret (client_credentials grant, ORCE_NOTARY_CLIENT_ID / ORCE_NOTARY_CLIENT_SECRET) and calls DCS with the resulting access token. It holds no wallet, presents no verifiable credential, and mints nothing itself - what it may do is fixed by the DCS deployment (systemClients in the Helm values), not asked for in the token.

What is fetched is safe to forward: the head is only hashes, counts and an RFC 3161 token - no contract DIDs, no organization identities, no event payloads. Because every root chains to its predecessor, ONE stored head pins the entire log anchored before it, so a 15 minute cadence is enough.

name: Build client_credentials request
GET /pac/audit/checkpoint/head
Take only a new head
